question.text: A fund invested INR 200 crore, distributed INR 150 crore, and has a residual portfolio valued at INR 180 crore. The RVPI is:

explanation.text: **The Logic:** RVPI = Residual Portfolio Value / Paid-In Capital = 180 / 200 = 0.90x. The unrealized portfolio is worth 90% of the original investment. TVPI = DPI + RVPI = (150/200) + (180/200) = 0.75 + 0.90 = 1.65x. **The Trap:** Don't add distributions to residual value when calculating RVPI — it only measures the unrealized portion. TVPI adds both DPI and RVPI together. **Cross-Exam Context:** RVPI calculations are tested in XIX-A. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** This fund has returned 75% of capital (DPI 0.75x) while still holding 90% in unrealized value — a strong position. If even half the residual converts to DPI through exits, the total return will be excellent (1.65x+ TVPI).
